description: A Python meeting scheduling library (`meeting_scheduler`) used by a remote-first calendar application is causing double-bookings and scheduling meetings at 3 AM for international team members. The library fails to correctly handle time zone conversions when validating 'working hours' (9 AM - 5 PM local time) and contains a logic error in checking for overlapping time slots. The project is located at `/home/ga/PycharmProjects/meeting_scheduler`. Resolve the issues in `scheduler/core.py` so that `check_overlap` correctly detects enclosing intervals, `is_working_hours` converts meeting times to the user's local timezone before checking bounds, and `validate_future_time` correctly compares timezone-aware datetimes. All 18 tests must pass without modifications to the tests or data models.
